---
title: "Partitioned labeling and deterministic reduction"
engine: pi-bio
format: gfm
---

This pattern isolates the mechanics often associated with RLM-style long-context work. Unlabeled rows are partitioned
across separate processes; each process applies a deterministic SQL stand-in for semantic labeling; the host merges
the returned artifacts and performs an exact reduction. There is no model recursion or quality claim.

```{ts .pi-bio}
import assert from "node:assert/strict";
import { spawn } from "node:child_process";
import { DuckDBInstance } from "@duckdb/node-api";

const context = [
  "How many moons orbit Mars", "Who painted the ceiling", "Where is the capital city", "Define a covalent bond",
  "How old is the universe", "Who invented calypso", "Where is the river", "What planet is red",
  "How many years in a decade", "Who wrote the play", "Define entropy", "Where do salmon spawn",
].map((instance, index) => ({ id: index + 1, instance }));
const workerSource = `
import { DuckDBInstance } from "@duckdb/node-api";
let input = "";
for await (const chunk of process.stdin) input += chunk;
const rows = JSON.parse(input);
const instance = await DuckDBInstance.create(":memory:");
const connection = await instance.connect();
await connection.run("CREATE TABLE part(id INTEGER, instance VARCHAR)");
for (const row of rows) await connection.run("INSERT INTO part VALUES (?, ?)", [row.id, row.instance]);
const labels = (await connection.runAndReadAll(\`
  SELECT id, CASE
    WHEN lower(instance) LIKE '%how many%' OR lower(instance) LIKE '%how old%' THEN 'number'
    WHEN lower(instance) LIKE '%who%' THEN 'human'
    WHEN lower(instance) LIKE '%where%' THEN 'location'
    WHEN lower(instance) LIKE '%define%' THEN 'description'
    ELSE 'entity' END AS label
  FROM part ORDER BY id
\`)).getRowObjects().map((row) => ({ id: Number(row.id), label: row.label }));
process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify({ pid: process.pid, labels }));
connection.closeSync();
instance.closeSync();
`;
const runWorker = (partition) => new Promise((resolveWorker, reject) => {
  const child = spawn(process.execPath, ["--input-type=module", "--eval", workerSource], {
    cwd: process.cwd(), stdio: ["pipe", "pipe", "pipe"],
  });
  let stdout = "";
  let stderr = "";
  child.stdout.on("data", (chunk) => { stdout += chunk; });
  child.stderr.on("data", (chunk) => { stderr += chunk; });
  child.on("error", reject);
  child.on("close", (code) => code === 0 ? resolveWorker(JSON.parse(stdout)) : reject(new Error(stderr)));
  child.stdin.end(JSON.stringify(partition));
});

const partitionSize = Math.ceil(context.length / 3);
const partitions = Array.from({ length: 3 }, (_, index) => context.slice(index * partitionSize, (index + 1) * partitionSize));
const workers = await Promise.all(partitions.map(runWorker));
assert.equal(new Set(workers.map((worker) => worker.pid)).size, 3);
const labels = workers.flatMap((worker) => worker.labels);

const instance = await DuckDBInstance.create(":memory:");
const connection = await instance.connect();
await connection.run("CREATE TABLE labels(id INTEGER, label VARCHAR)");
for (const row of labels) await connection.run("INSERT INTO labels VALUES (?, ?)", [row.id, row.label]);
const counts = (await connection.runAndReadAll("SELECT label, count(*) AS n FROM labels GROUP BY label ORDER BY label"))
  .getRowObjects().map((row) => ({ label: row.label, n: Number(row.n) }));
connection.closeSync();
instance.closeSync();
assert.deepEqual(counts, [
  { label: "description", n: 2 },
  { label: "entity", n: 1 },
  { label: "human", n: 3 },
  { label: "location", n: 3 },
  { label: "number", n: 3 },
]);
piBio.json({
  pattern: "partitioned-labeling-reduce",
  partitions: partitions.map((partition) => partition.map((row) => row.id)),
  labels,
  counts,
});
```

The executable result proves process isolation, artifact merge, and deterministic reduction. A real RLM experiment
must replace the stand-in map with recursive model calls and compare quality and budget against a baseline.
